This bead OUTPUTS the canonical TD doc that the 5 hygiene beads + the 3 sibling reconciliation beads reference. NO code or schema changes in this bead — those are separate sub-beads.

## Critical constraint (per ADR-004)

The bead-record schema MUST stay compatible with the bd/br interchange contract. Status enum is FIXED at the bd/br canonical 6 (open, in_progress, closed, blocked, proposed, cancelled). DDx-specific execution semantics live in labels, events, or Extra map — NOT in new statuses.

## Today's actual state (verified)

- bead-record.schema.json enum: 6 values (the bd/br canonical set)
- FEAT-004 line 65 docs: 3 values (lags schema)
- Persisted reality: 'ddx bead list --json | jq -r .[].status | sort -u' shows only open/in_progress/closed in active rows today
- Code uses additional names (done, needs_human, pending, ready, review, needs_investigation) but these are NOT persisted statuses — event kinds, terminal phases, derived queue categories, or labels

## Five in-flight hygiene beads each touch state-machine vocabulary (must align with this TD)

- ddx-b24e9630 (no_changes verification): NoChangesContract subsection
- ddx-3c154349 (auto-triage): TriageContract subsection
- ddx-aede917d (drain pause on quota): QuotaPauseContract subsection
- ddx-c6e3db02 (rate-limit retry): RateLimitRetryContract subsection
- ddx-da11a34a (lock contention): LockContentionContract subsection

## Scope (this bead authors the doc only)

Author docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-NNN-bead-state-machine.md with:

1. Distinguish category of every observed name as one of: persisted bead status (locked to bd/br set) / derived queue category / event kind / terminal phase / claim metadata / label / Extra metadata field / worker state.
2. Persisted status enumeration — confirm the bd/br-compatible 6-value set. Status enum is NOT modified.
3. Transition matrix — table or diagram: which status → which is allowed; who can drive each (operator-via-CLI, drain loop, agent, reviewer); what events fire on each transition.
4. Event vocabulary — unified across hygiene beads (no_changes_*, drain-paused-quota, etc.).
5. Outcome → label/event/Extra mapping — when execute-bead returns merged/already_satisfied/review_block/execution_failed/no_changes_*, the resulting bead state changes (status transition within the 6, label add/remove, event append, Extra field update).
6. Claim semantics — when claimed, when released, stale claim handling.
7. Naming-role decision matrix — every observed name labeled with role + rationale: blocked, in_progress, open, closed, cancelled, proposed (current schema 6), done, pending, ready, review (in code as non-status), needs_human (label today; stays label), needs_investigation (LABEL per bd/br compatibility, NOT status), blocked-on-upstream:<id> (label).
8. Per-hygiene-bead contract subsection — one per bead (NoChangesContract, TriageContract, QuotaPauseContract, RateLimitRetryContract, LockContentionContract): status transitions used (within canonical 6), labels added/removed, events fired, claim behavior, loop interactions.
9. Future-change process — explicit rule: any bead introducing or changing labels/events/outcome semantics/claim handling MUST cite the TD section authorizing it. Adding a new persisted status requires upstream bd/br coordination + ADR-004 amendment.
10. Worker-state enumeration — parallel section: idle / draining / paused-quota / paused-rate-limit / exiting. Worker-state is distinct from bead-state, owned by worker process not bead store.
11. Migration plan placeholder — say 'sibling bead handles survey + execution' (the actual survey runs in the sibling bead).

## What does NOT land here

- Schema/code/docs reconciliation (sibling bead handles)
- CI guard tests (sibling bead handles)
- Hygiene-bead AC TD-NNN substitution + migration survey (sibling bead handles)",

270	## 8. Per-Hygiene-Bead Contracts
271	
272	Each hygiene bead's AC must cite this section. Anything not in the
273	contract here is out of scope for that bead.
274	
275	### 8.1 NoChangesContract (ddx-b24e9630)
276	
277	Status transitions used:
278	
279	- `in_progress → closed` for `no_changes_decomposed` (terminal: children
280	  filed).
281	- `in_progress → blocked` for `no_changes_blocked` (operator follow-up
282	  expected).
283	- `in_progress → open` for `no_changes_no_evidence` and
284	  `no_changes_recoverable` (claim released, bead returns to the queue).
285	
286	Labels added:
287	
288	- `decomposed` (on `no_changes_decomposed`).
289	- `needs_human` (on `no_changes_blocked`).
290	- `triage` (on `no_changes_no_evidence`).
291	
292	Labels removed: none.
293	
294	Events fired: one of the four `no_changes_*` event kinds from section 4.
295	
296	Claim behavior: the claim is released on every outcome except
297	`no_changes_decomposed` and `no_changes_blocked`, where the bead leaves
298	`in_progress` directly to a terminal/blocked status.
299	
300	Loop interaction: the drain loop reads the outcome, applies the mapping
301	in section 5, and appends the matching event. It does not invent new
302	event kinds.
303	
304	### 8.2 TriageContract (ddx-3c154349)
305	
306	Status transitions used:
307	
308	- `in_progress → open` when releasing stale claims.
309	- `open → blocked` when triage decides a bead has an unmet hard
310	  precondition.
311	
312	Labels added: `triage`, `needs_human`, `blocked-on-upstream:<id>` (as
313	applicable).
314	
315	Labels removed: `triage` after a triaged bead is reclaimed cleanly (the
316	reverse mutation of `triage` is "next successful claim removes the
317	label").
318	
319	Events fired: `auto-triage`, `unclaimed`, optionally `blocked`.
320	
321	Claim behavior: the auto-triage sweep is the only path that releases a
322	claim it does not own. It MUST log the prior `assignee` and `claimed-at`
323	in the appended event body.
324	
325	Loop interaction: triage runs out-of-band of execute-bead. It MUST NOT
326	race with an active drain attempt holding the store lock — it acquires
327	the same store lock and releases-then-reclaims is not its job.
328	
329	### 8.3 QuotaPauseContract (ddx-aede917d)
330	
331	Status transitions used: none. Quota is a worker-state concern, not a
332	bead-state concern.
333	
334	Labels: none added on the affected bead. The current attempt is treated
335	as `execution_failed` (claim released, status returns to `open`).
336	
337	Events fired: `drain-paused-quota` is appended to a worker-scoped event

344	Loop interaction: the worker transitions to worker-state

131	| `open` → `in_progress` | yes | drain loop or operator (claim) | `claimed` |
132	| `open` → `blocked` | yes | operator or auto-triage | `blocked` |
133	| `open` → `cancelled` | yes | operator | `cancelled` |
134	| `in_progress` → `open` | yes | unclaim (operator or stale-claim sweep) | `unclaimed` |
135	| `in_progress` → `closed` | yes | drain loop (on merge/already-satisfied) or operator | `closed-merged` / `closed-already-satisfied` |
136	| `in_progress` → `blocked` | yes | drain loop on `review_block`, or operator | `blocked-review` / `blocked` |
137	| `blocked` → `open` | yes | operator (block resolved) | `unblocked` |
138	| `blocked` → `cancelled` | yes | operator | `cancelled` |

140	| `cancelled` → * | no | — | — (cancelled is terminal) |
141	| any → `proposed` | no | — | proposed is an entry-only status |
142	
143	Closed and cancelled are terminal. Re-opening a closed bead is not a
144	transition; it is filing a follow-up bead with `replaces` set.
145	
146	## 4. Event Vocabulary (unified)
147	
148	Events are append-only entries on `Extra["events"]` (see TD-004 evidence
149	model). The kinds below form the closed vocabulary used across the five
150	hygiene beads. New kinds require updating this section.
151	
152	Lifecycle events:
153	
154	- `triaged` — `proposed → open`
155	- `claimed` — claim acquired
156	- `unclaimed` — claim released
157	- `blocked` — moved to `blocked` (carries a reason in `body`)
158	- `unblocked` — moved off `blocked`
159	- `closed-merged` — drain loop closed after a merge
160	- `closed-already-satisfied` — drain loop detected the work was already done
161	- `closed` — operator-driven close (catch-all)
162	- `cancelled` — moved to `cancelled`
163	
164	Drain-outcome events (no_changes family — NoChangesContract):
165	
166	- `no_changes_decomposed` — agent decomposed the bead instead of changing
167	  files
168	- `no_changes_blocked` — agent declared no_changes with a justified blocker
169	- `no_changes_no_evidence` — agent exited without commit and without a
170	  rationale file
171	- `no_changes_recoverable` — transient cause, will retry
172	
173	Drain-control events:
174	
175	- `drain-paused-quota` — QuotaPauseContract: drain paused because the active
176	  harness reported quota exhaustion
177	- `drain-resumed-quota`
178	- `rate-limit-retry` — RateLimitRetryContract: a single attempt was retried
179	  after a rate-limit response
180	- `lock-contention` — LockContentionContract: store-lock contention was
181	  observed and handled (retry or backoff)
182	
183	Review and triage events:
184	
185	- `review-block` — reviewer raised a BLOCKING finding
186	- `review-pass` — reviewer cleared the change
187	- `auto-triage` — TriageContract: triage path mutated labels/status
188	
189	Each event SHOULD include `kind`, `actor`, `created_at`, and a free-form
190	`body` (TD-004 schema). Drain events SHOULD additionally include the
191	`run-id` of the execution attempt in `body` or in a structured `extra`
192	field.
193	
194	## 5. Outcome → Label / Event / Extra Mapping
195	
196	When a drain attempt finishes, `execute-bead` returns one of a fixed set of
197	outcomes. The mapping below is normative.
198	
199	| Outcome | Status transition | Label changes | Events appended | Extra updates |
200	|---|---|---|---|---|
201	| `merged` | `in_progress → closed` | remove `claimed`, add `last-merged-rev:<sha>` (optional) | `closed-merged` | `Extra["last-run"]` updated with run-id |
202	| `already_satisfied` | `in_progress → closed` | (none) | `closed-already-satisfied` | `Extra["last-run"]` |
203	| `review_block` | `in_progress → blocked` | add `needs_human` (label) | `review-block` | `Extra["last-review"]` carries findings ref |
204	| `execution_failed` | `in_progress → open` (claim released) | (none) | `unclaimed` + a structured failure event | `Extra["last-run"]` |
205	| `no_changes_decomposed` | `in_progress → closed` | add `decomposed` | `no_changes_decomposed` | `Extra["children"]` lists child IDs |
206	| `no_changes_blocked` | `in_progress → blocked` | add `needs_human` | `no_changes_blocked` | `Extra["last-rationale"]` |
207	| `no_changes_no_evidence` | `in_progress → open` | add `triage` | `no_changes_no_evidence` | (none) |
208	| `no_changes_recoverable` | `in_progress → open` | (none) | `no_changes_recoverable` | (none) |
209	
210	`needs_human` is a **label**, not a status. It is the standard signal that
211	a human operator should look at the bead before drain re-attempts it.
212	
213	## 6. Claim Semantics
214	
215	Claim is metadata, not status. The persisted-status convention is that
216	`in_progress` implies a claim is held; `open` implies no claim. The actual
217	claim fields are:
218	
219	- `Extra["assignee"]` (string) — the entity holding the claim.
220	- `Extra["claimed-at"]` (RFC3339 timestamp) — when the claim was acquired.
221	- `Extra["claimed-pid"]` (int) — process id of the claim holder, advisory.
222	
223	Acquired:
224	
225	- During `open → in_progress` transition under the store lock.
226	- The drain loop and operator CLI (`ddx bead update --claim`) are the two
227	  legitimate drivers.
228	
229	Released:
230	
231	- Explicit `--unclaim` (or implicit unclaim on `in_progress → open`).
232	- On terminal close (`closed` / `cancelled`) the claim metadata is left in
233	  place as historical record but is no longer authoritative.
234	
235	Stale claim handling (TriageContract owns the policy):
236	
237	- A claim is *stale* if `claimed-at` is older than the configured stale
238	  threshold AND the bead is still `in_progress`.
239	- Stale claims are released by the auto-triage path (`auto-triage` event),
240	  status moves `in_progress → open`, and the bead becomes queue-eligible
241	  again.
242	- Auto-triage MUST NOT delete prior claim metadata; it appends an event
243	  recording the release.
244	
245	## 7. Naming-Role Decision Matrix
246	
247	Every name observed in code, schema, docs, or persisted data is assigned
248	a single category here. Names not in the persisted-status set MUST NOT
249	appear as `status` values.
250	
251	| Name | Category | Rationale |
252	|---|---|---|
253	| `open` | Persisted status | bd/br canonical; queue-eligible default. |
254	| `in_progress` | Persisted status | bd/br canonical; implies an active claim. |
255	| `closed` | Persisted status | bd/br canonical; terminal-success path. |
256	| `blocked` | Persisted status | bd/br canonical; hard precondition unmet. |
257	| `proposed` | Persisted status | bd/br canonical; pre-triage backlog. |
258	| `cancelled` | Persisted status | bd/br canonical; terminal-abandoned path. |
259	| `done` | Removed alias | Historical alias of `closed` in code paths. Not a persisted status. The reconciliation sibling bead replaces remaining code references with `closed`. |

18	// ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime carries the non-serializable plumbing and
19	// per-invocation runtime intent for an execute-bead loop run. Durable knobs
20	// (assignee, retry caps, harness/model, tier bounds, etc.) live on
21	// config.ResolvedConfig and are passed via Run's rcfg argument.
22	type ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime struct {
23		Log          io.Writer
24		EventSink    io.Writer
25		ProgressCh   chan<- ProgressEvent
26		PreClaimHook func(ctx context.Context) error
27		// RoutePreflight, when non-nil, is invoked between nextCandidate and
28		// Claim. It is expected to call upstream ResolveRoute against the
29		// loop's resolved (harness, model) and return whatever typed routing
30		// error the upstream surfaced — notably agent.ErrHarnessModelIncompatible
31		// when the harness allow-list rejects the model. Any non-nil error
32		// causes the loop to exit immediately without claiming the bead, with
33		// a worker-level execution_failed record naming the rejected pair.
34		// DDx does NOT duplicate the upstream allow-list; this gate only
35		// consumes the typed-incompatibility surface.
36		RoutePreflight func(ctx context.Context, harness, model string) error
37		Once           bool
38		PollInterval   time.Duration
39		NoReview       bool
40		LabelFilter    string
41		SessionID      string
42		WorkerID       string
43		ProjectRoot    string
44	}

46	// DefaultReviewMaxRetries is the number of reviewer attempts allowed per
47	// committed result_rev before the loop emits a terminal
48	// `review-manual-required` event and stops re-executing primary. FEAT-022 §14.
49	const DefaultReviewMaxRetries = 3
50	
51	// MaxLoopCooldown is the absolute upper bound the execute-loop will set for
52	// any execute-loop-retry-after value. Year-scale parks effectively mean
53	// "never retry" and that should be a deliberate operator choice via
54	// `ddx bead update --set execute-loop-retry-after=...`, not an automatic
55	// loop decision. Beyond this cap, the loop refuses to lengthen the cooldown
56	// further; an operator extending it manually still works.
57	const MaxLoopCooldown = 24 * time.Hour
58	
59	// LandConflictCooldown is the short cooldown applied when all conflict-recovery
60	// paths fail (3-way ort auto-merge + optional focused-resolve agent) and the
61	// bead is parked as land_conflict_unresolvable. Much shorter than
62	// MaxLoopCooldown because land conflicts often resolve quickly as sibling beads
63	// advance the base branch.
64	const LandConflictCooldown = 15 * time.Minute
65	
66	// StoreErrorCooldown is the short cooldown applied when a Store.* operation in
67	// the outcome-handling block returns a transient error. It prevents an
68	// immediate re-queue of the affected bead while allowing the loop to continue
69	// to the next candidate. Store errors during outcome handling are non-fatal:
70	// they are logged and emitted as loop-error events but do not terminate the
71	// worker.
72	const StoreErrorCooldown = 5 * time.Minute
73	
74	// handleOutcomeStoreError is called when a Store.* operation in the
75	// post-Execute outcome block returns a non-nil error. It logs the error,
76	// appends a kind:loop-error event to the bead (best-effort), sets a short
77	// cooldown so the bead is not immediately re-queued, increments
78	// result.Failures, and returns true — signalling the caller to continue to
79	// the next loop iteration rather than returning.
80	//
81	// If ctx is already cancelled, it returns false so the caller can propagate
82	// the cancellation immediately.
83	func (w *ExecuteBeadWorker) handleOutcomeStoreError(ctx context.Context, beadID, operation string, storeErr error, assignee string, result *ExecuteBeadLoopResult, runtime ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime, now func() time.Time) bool {
84		if ctx.Err() != nil {
85			return false
86		}
87		if runtime.Log != nil {
88			_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(runtime.Log, "outcome store error (%s %s): %v (continuing)\n", operation, beadID, storeErr)
89		}
90		_ = w.Store.AppendEvent(beadID, bead.BeadEvent{
91			Kind:      "loop-error",
92			Summary:   operation + " failed",
93			Body:      storeErr.Error(),
94			Actor:     assignee,
95			Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
96			CreatedAt: now().UTC(),
97		})
98		cooldownUntil := now().UTC().Add(StoreErrorCooldown)
99		_ = w.Store.SetExecutionCooldown(beadID, cooldownUntil, "loop-error", operation+": "+storeErr.Error())
100		result.Failures++
101		result.LastFailureStatus = "loop-error"
102		return true
103	}
104	
105	// capLoopCooldown clamps a loop-set cooldown duration to MaxLoopCooldown.
106	// The loop uses this for every SetExecutionCooldown call so no automatic
107	// path can silently park a bead for a year.
108	func capLoopCooldown(d time.Duration) time.Duration {
109		if d <= 0 {
110			return 0
111		}
112		if d > MaxLoopCooldown {
113			return MaxLoopCooldown
114		}
115		return d
116	}

276	// ExecuteBeadWorker drains the current single-project execution-ready queue.
277	// It intentionally does not retry a failed/conflicted bead again in the same
278	// process run; a later operator-driven invocation can create the next attempt.
279	type ExecuteBeadWorker struct {
280		Store               ExecuteBeadLoopStore
281		Executor            ExecuteBeadExecutor
282		SatisfactionChecker SatisfactionChecker // nil → count-based default
283		Now                 func() time.Time
284		// Reviewer, when non-nil, is called after every successful merge to
285		// validate the commit against the bead's acceptance criteria. When nil,
286		// post-merge review is skipped (same behaviour as --no-review).
287		Reviewer BeadReviewer
288	
289		// ConflictResolver, when non-nil, is called after the 3-way ort auto-merge
290		// step fails to recover a preserved iteration. The implementation should
291		// attempt a focused conflict-resolution pass (e.g. a cheap-tier agent run
292		// with the conflict files and bead AC) and return the new merged tip SHA on
293		// success. isBlocking=true signals the conflict requires human judgment
294		// (escalating to land_conflict_needs_human); false means failed-but-retriable.
295		ConflictResolver func(ctx context.Context, beadID, preserveRef, projectRoot string) (newTip string, isBlocking bool, err error)
296	
297		// ComplexityGate, when non-nil, is invoked between RoutePreflight and Claim.
298		// It evaluates whether the candidate bead is atomic (proceed to Claim),
299		// decomposable (file children, block parent, re-pick), or ambiguous

865	// TestExecuteBeadWorkerNoChangesDoesNotStarveQueue verifies that a bead with
866	// repeated no_changes results cannot prevent other ready beads from being
867	// executed across multiple queue passes. It also verifies the full adjudication
868	// lifecycle: other beads run unblocked while the no-changes bead is open, and
869	// the no-changes bead is eventually closed as already_satisfied at the threshold.
870	func TestExecuteBeadWorkerNoChangesDoesNotStarveQueue(t *testing.T) {
871		store := bead.NewStore(t.TempDir())
872		require.NoError(t, store.Init())
873	
874		// Three beads: one that always returns no_changes (no cooldown), two that succeed.
875		// Not supplying BaseRev/ResultRev means shouldSuppressNoProgress returns false,
876		// so no retry-after is written and the bead stays immediately retryable between
877		// passes. This keeps the test deterministic without mocking time.
878		ncBead := &bead.Bead{ID: "ddx-nc10", Title: "Always no-changes", Priority: 0}
879		work1 := &bead.Bead{ID: "ddx-wk11", Title: "Work bead 1", Priority: 1}
880		work2 := &bead.Bead{ID: "ddx-wk12", Title: "Work bead 2", Priority: 2}
881		require.NoError(t, store.Create(ncBead))
882		require.NoError(t, store.Create(work1))
883		require.NoError(t, store.Create(work2))
884

880		work2 := &bead.Bead{ID: "ddx-wk12", Title: "Work bead 2", Priority: 2}
881		require.NoError(t, store.Create(ncBead))
882		require.NoError(t, store.Create(work1))
883		require.NoError(t, store.Create(work2))
884	
885		var mu sync.Mutex
886		executed := []string{}
887	
888		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
889			Store: store,
890			Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
891				mu.Lock()
892				executed = append(executed, beadID)
893				mu.Unlock()
894				if beadID == ncBead.ID {
895					// No BaseRev/ResultRev → shouldSuppressNoProgress returns false
896					// → no cooldown written → bead is immediately retryable.
897					return ExecuteBeadReport{
898						BeadID: beadID,
899						Status: ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges,
900					}, nil
901				}
902				return ExecuteBeadReport{
903					BeadID:    beadID,
904					Status:    ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess,
905					SessionID: "sess-" + beadID,
906					ResultRev: "bbbb",
907				}, nil
908			}),
909		}
910	
911		const threshold = 2
912		cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{
913			Assignee:                "worker",
914			MaxNoChangesBeforeClose: threshold,
915		}
916		rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
917		runtime := ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{}
918	
919		// Pass 1: all three beads are ready.
920		// ncBead returns no_changes (count=1 < threshold) and stays open.
921		// work1 and work2 succeed and are closed.
922		// The `attempted` map inside Run prevents ncBead from being picked up
923		// a second time within the same pass — the other beads run unblocked.
924		result1, err := worker.Run(context.Background(), rcfg, runtime)
925		require.NoError(t, err)
926		assert.Equal(t, 3, result1.Attempts, "all three beads must be attempted in pass 1")
927		assert.Equal(t, 2, result1.Successes)
928		assert.Equal(t, 1, result1.Failures)
929		assert.Equal(t, ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges, result1.LastFailureStatus)
930	
931		w1, _ := store.Get(work1.ID)
932		w2, _ := store.Get(work2.ID)
933		nc, _ := store.Get(ncBead.ID)
934		assert.Equal(t, bead.StatusClosed, w1.Status)
935		assert.Equal(t, bead.StatusClosed, w2.Status)
936		assert.Equal(t, bead.StatusOpen, nc.Status, "ncBead must stay open after first no_changes")
937	
938		// Pass 2: only ncBead remains; count reaches threshold → closed as already_satisfied.
939		result2, err := worker.Run(context.Background(), rcfg, runtime)
940		require.NoError(t, err)
941		assert.Equal(t, 1, result2.Attempts)
942		assert.Equal(t, 1, result2.Successes, "ncBead must be closed as already_satisfied")

    project root (60s timeout). Exit 0 → bead closed as already_satisfied
    with a no_changes_verified event. Non-zero → triage:no-changes-unverified
    label + no_changes_unverified event, bead stays open.
  - status: needs_investigation + reason: <why> — triage:needs-investigation
    label + no_changes_needs_investigation event, bead stays open.

Bare rationales (no marker) are rejected: triage:no-changes-unjustified
label + no_changes_unjustified event, bead stays open. The legacy
rationaleIsSpecific / count-based heuristic is no longer the default
adjudication path; SatisfactionChecker remains as an explicit escape
valve. Status enum stays in the bd/br canonical 6 — all triage state
lives in labels and events (ADR-004).

Execute-bead prompt instructions updated to require one of the markers
in any no_changes_rationale.txt the agent writes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

data.text:
Committed b543f08. Full agent test suite passed in pre-commit (106s).

Summary of changes:
- **New file** `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_no_changes_verify.go` — `ParseNoChangesRationale`, marker constants (`NoChangesKindVerified/NeedsInvestigation/Unjustified`), event names (`no_changes_verified/unverified/unjustified/needs_investigation`), label names (`triage:no-changes-unverified/unjustified/needs-investigation`), and `DefaultVerificationCommandRunner` with 60s timeout.
- **Loop** (`execute_bead_loop.go`) — replaced default no_changes adjudication with `adjudicateNoChangesContract`. SatisfactionChecker remains as escape valve. New `addBeadLabel` helper. Loop emits the contract event in addition to the existing executeBeadLoopEvent.
- **Prompt** (`execute_bead.go`) — added `instrNoChangesContract` block to both Claude and Agent prompt variants requiring one of the markers.
- **Tests** — added 4 new contract-path tests (verified→close, unverified/unjustified/needs-investigation→stay-open+label) plus parser/runner unit tests; updated 7 legacy tests that depended on count-based heuristic to use the new marker syntax.
- bd/br schema_compat tests still pass; status enum unchanged.
